name: Bread Stuffed w/ Green, Cheese, Peppers, and Olives
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 Cups Semolina Flour (may require additional 1 cup)
6 Cups Unbleached Bread Flour (may require additional 1/2 cup, plus extra for rolling)
1 1/4 Cups Mixed-Grain Flour (a mixture of barley, rye, and oats)
1/4 Cup Grape Must Syrup or Molasses
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
1 Pound Onions - to yield 1 cup cooked onions
3 Tablespoons Olive Oil
1 Teaspoon Sugar
1 1/4 Pounds Curly Endive (or any greens of your choice) - steamed, drained and chopped
1 Cup Roasted Sweet Peppers (from 1 1/4 lb sweet peppers)
1/2 Pound Greek Olives (pitted and roughly chopped)
1 Cup Feta Cheese
3 Cloves Garlic
1/4 Cup Flat Leaf Parsley - finely chopped
1/4 Cup Fresh Dill - finely chopped
2 Tablespoons Fresh Mint - finely chopped
1 Teaspoon Rice or Cracked Wheat (optional)
To make the bread dough:
1. Put the dry yeast in a cup with 1/2 cup hot water (just above body temperature, to your finger). Stir in sugar and leave in a warm undisturbed place to prove.
2. Mix semolina flour (or unbleached bread flour) with whole wheat and mixed-grain flours, adding salt. Pour onto a work surface, making a hollow in the center.
3. When the yeast is frothy and doubled in bulk, pour it into the center, with olive oil, molasses, and 1 cup warm water. Mix together to make a cohesive dough, adding more water as needed.
4. Knead the dough, keeping the work surface, hands, and dough floured, until the dough is velvety and elastic - about 25 minutes. Cover with a damp cloth and leave to rise until almost double in bulk - about an hour.
To make the filling:
1. Sauté chopped onions in olive oil, adding little splashes of water if they stick. When golden brown, sprinkle onions with sugar and cook until caramelized.
2. Assemble greens and roasted peppers, chopping them roughly. Drain all cooked vegetables thoroughly. Rinse olives if necessary.
3. Make herbed cheese by mixing feta with garlic, parsley, dill, and mint.
To assemble:
1. When the dough has risen, punch it down and divide into two slightly unequal pieces.
2. Pat or roll each piece into a circle about 3/4-inch thick. Place larger circle on an oiled or non-stick baking sheet.
3. Cover both halves and leave to rise in a warm place for about 30 minutes.
4. If the filling ingredients are moist, sprinkle dough laid out on the baking sheet with rice or cracked wheat.
5. Layer fillings (in order of preference) on the dough, leaving a border of 2-2 1/2 inches all around the edge.
6. Lay second circle gently over the filling.
7. Moisten outer edges of bottom and roll them around and over the edges of top. Press to seal.
8. Cut diagonal slits in the top for steam escape. Leave dough to rise until almost doubled in bulk - about 1 hour.
9. Bake in an oven preheated to 350°F for 1 hour, or until a hollow sound is heard when tapped on the bottom.
